# Progress

Shows measurable completion for a task or process.

## Installation

Add the Progress component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/progress
```

## Usage

Use Progress when a process has a meaningful completion percentage. Pass the current value with `:model-value` and update it from the task's real state. The local indicator styling expects a value from 0 to 100.

**Progress example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-md flex-col gap-3">
    <div class="flex items-center justify-between text-sm">
      <span id="package-upload-label">Uploading package</span>
      <span class="text-muted-foreground">{{ value }}%</span>
    </div>
    <Progress :model-value="value" aria-labelledby="package-upload-label" />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Progress } from '@/Components/Progress'

const value = ref(68)
</script>
```

## Labels and values

Name the task and show the percentage in text when either matters to the user. Associate that text with the progress bar using `aria-labelledby`, or provide an `aria-label` when no visible label is available. Do not label every bar only “Progress”; say what is progressing.

```vue
<div class="flex items-center justify-between">
  <span id="upload-label">Uploading report</span>
  <span>{{ uploadProgress }}%</span>
</div>
<Progress
  :model-value="uploadProgress"
  aria-labelledby="upload-label"
/>
```

## Controlled values

Progress is read-only feedback, even though its value is controlled by application state. Clamp unexpected values before passing them and move to 100 only when the work is actually complete. Do not let users drag or click a Progress bar; use Slider when they are choosing a numeric value.

```vue
<Progress :model-value="safeProgress" aria-label="Importing contacts" />

<script setup lang="ts">
import { computed, ref } from 'vue'

const progress = ref(0)
const safeProgress = computed(() => Math.min(100, Math.max(0, progress.value)))
</script>
```

## Known and unknown duration

Use Progress only when the amount completed can be estimated honestly. A bar that advances arbitrarily or repeatedly stalls at a near-complete value creates false precision. For work with unknown duration, use a Spinner with concise loading text instead.

> **Use Spinner for indeterminate work: [Spinner](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/spinner)**
>
> Spinner communicates that work is active without claiming a percentage. Use Skeleton instead when an entire content shape is waiting to load.

## Status and announcements

Progress communicates state; it does not perform or cancel an action. Place pause or cancel controls beside it as separately labeled Buttons. The progress semantics expose the current value, but changing the bar is not a substitute for announcing a final success or failure.

Avoid putting every percentage update in an `aria-live` region, which can overwhelm assistive technology. Announce meaningful milestones or the final outcome in a separate, concise status message when necessary.

Hosted documentation: [Progress](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/progress)
